reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate — {brand}
reauth_confirm.description:
The session for **{username}** has expired.

Please re-enter your password.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure VW Group Connect
reconfigure.description: Change credentials or settings without removing the integration.

mfa.title: Two-factor confirmation
mfa.description:
A confirmation code has been sent to **{username}**.

Enter the code from your email or authenticator app.

data_description.mfa_code: 6-digit code — valid for a few minutes.

email_password.title: Sign in with email + password (legacy)
email_password.description:
Connect your vehicle to Home Assistant.

Choose your brand and enter the credentials from your manufacturer's app.

data_description.brand: Choose your brand — myAudi, WeConnect ID, MyŠkoda, SEAT, CUPRA, VW US/CA or Porsche.
data_description.username: Email address from the manufacturer app.
data_description.spin: Only needed for locking. App → Security → S-PIN.
data_description.scan_interval: How often data is fetched. Default: 5 min. Minimum: 3 min.
data_description.force_enable_access: For older vehicles that fail the capability check.

user.title: Set up VW Group Connect
user.description:
How would you like to sign in?

**Browser-Login (recommended)** — open a URL on your phone or laptop, sign in to your Brand ID account, approve the device. No password is ever stored in Home Assistant. Available for Audi, Škoda, SEAT and CUPRA.

**Email + Password (legacy)** — enter your Brand ID credentials directly. Required for Volkswagen EU and Porsche.

menu_options.mbb_login: Volkswagen EU — Durable Login (MBB, two-way alpha)

mbb_login.title: Volkswagen EU — Durable Login (MBB, ALPHA)
mbb_login.description: **Experimental, opt-in — Volkswagen EU only.** A browser confirmation (no password stored in Home Assistant) mints a durable, refreshable token via the legacy MBB backend. Unlike the read-only portal, this survives restarts and supports two-way remote lock/unlock. **Enter your VIN** (the MBB token can't list your garage by itself), and add your S-PIN to enable lock/unlock. On the next screen you will see a short code and a URL to open on any device.

data_description.mbb_vins: Your 17-character VIN (on the windscreen / registration). Multiple cars: separate with a comma. Required — the durable MBB token works per-VIN but can't enumerate your garage.
data_description.spin: Optional 4-digit Security-PIN — required for remote lock/unlock.
data_description.force_enable_access: Force door-status entities even when the vehicle reports the data missing.

website_authproxy.title: Volkswagen.de website (BETA, read-only)
website_authproxy.description: **Experimental, opt-in.** Reads your Volkswagen via the volkswagen.de website login instead of the app backend. Volkswagen only, read-only (no remote commands). Sign in with your Volkswagen ID email and password. If your account uses email verification you will be asked for the code next.

data_description.username: The email of your Volkswagen ID / myVolkswagen account.
data_description.scan_interval: How often data is fetched (minutes). Minimum: 5.

website_authproxy_otp.title: Email verification code
website_authproxy_otp.description:
A verification code was sent to the email for **{username}**.

Enter the code to finish signing in to the Volkswagen.de website (beta) channel.

data_description.mfa_code: Code from your email inbox — valid for a few minutes.

browser_login.title: Browser-Login — choose your brand
browser_login.description: Pick the brand of the vehicle you want to connect. On the next screen you will see a short code and a URL to visit on any device with a browser.

data_description.brand: Only brands with browser-login support are listed. Use 'Email + Password' for Volkswagen EU and Porsche.
data_description.force_enable_access: Force door-status entities even when the vehicle reports the data missing.
data_description.spin: Optional 4-digit Security-PIN for remote actions (lock, unlock, climate).

browser_login_pending.title: Requesting login code…
browser_login_pending.description: Asking the IDP for a one-time device code.

browser_login_approve.title: Approve in your browser
browser_login_approve.description: Scan the QR code with your phone, or open the URL on any device. Sign in to your Brand ID account if asked, confirm the code, then check the box below and click Submit.

data.qr_code: Scan with phone camera to open login page
data.verification_url: Or open this URL in any browser
data.user_code: Code to confirm in the browser
data.approved_in_browser: I have approved in the browser

data_description.qr_code: Point your phone camera at the QR code. The login page opens in your phone browser pre-filled with the code below.
data_description.verification_url: If you can't scan: copy this link, open it on any device, sign in to your Brand ID account.
data_description.user_code: After signing in, confirm this code in the browser to approve Home Assistant.
data_description.approved_in_browser: Leave checked. Click Submit only after you have signed in and confirmed the code in the browser.

error.cannot_connect: Connection failed. Check credentials and internet connection.
error.unknown: Unknown error. Please check the logs.
error.terms_and_conditions: Terms and conditions need to be accepted. Sign in to the app once and confirm.
error.marketing_consent: New privacy consent required. Open app: Profile → Consents.
error.two_factor_required: Two-factor authentication required. Sign in manually in the app and confirm the email code.
error.email_two_factor_required: Email 2FA required. Check your inbox (and spam) for a 6-digit code from VAG IDP, then sign in manually in the brand app once.
error.too_many_requests: Account temporarily suspended (too many login attempts). Please wait 15 minutes.
error.upstream_unavailable: VW backend temporarily unavailable. This is a server-side issue at VW, NOT a credentials problem. Please wait 5–15 minutes and try again. Do NOT reconfigure the integration.
error.brand_not_dag_eligible: Selected brand does not support browser login. Use the email + password flow instead.
error.still_waiting_browser: Still waiting for browser approval. Open the URL above, sign in, confirm the code, then click Submit again.

abort.mbb_not_eligible: Your vehicle isn't eligible for the MBB durable login — the backend returned "Unknown user". This means it's a newer ID / MEB model (ID.3/4/5, e-up, etc.) that was never enrolled in the legacy Car-Net/MBB system, so the durable login + remote commands can't reach it. Use the EU Data Act portal channel (or the Email + Password login) for these vehicles instead. The MBB login is for older Car-Net cars (most PHEV / combustion models).

progress.awaiting_browser_login: Open {verification_uri} in any browser, sign in with your Brand ID account, and confirm the code {user_code}.
progress.requesting_device_code: Requesting a one-time login code from the IDP…

data.force_ppe_climate: Audi PPE/PPC Climate body (Q6/A6 e-tron, RS e-tron GT Facelift, A3 2024+ PHEV)
data.wake_before_poll: Active vehicle wake-up before status poll
data.eu_data_act_auto_kickoff: EU Data Act: auto-kickoff Custom Data Request
data.enable_data_act_browser: EU Data Act portal: headless-browser fallback (EXPERIMENTAL)

data_description.scan_interval: How often data is fetched (minutes). Minimum: 3.
data_description.spin: S-PIN for door locking. App → Security → S-PIN.
data_description.enable_reverse_geocoding: Sends parking GPS to OpenStreetMap Nominatim to resolve street/city. Off by default for privacy.
data_description.read_only_mode: When enabled, only status sensors are created — no switches, buttons, locks, climate or sliders that send commands. Useful if you want vehicle telemetry without any risk of accidental actuation. Reload the integration after toggling.
data_description.force_ppe_climate: Audi-only. Forces the new PPE/PPC body shape (climatisationMode mandatory, targetTemperature omitted) on climate start. Enable only if you own a Q6 e-tron, A6 e-tron, RS e-tron GT Facelift or A3 2024+ PHEV — climate-start otherwise returns 400. Reload the integration after toggling.
data_description.enable_push_mqtt: EXPERIMENTAL: Skoda-only. Enables real-time MQTT push from Skoda mysmob backend (requires aiomqtt + firebase-messaging Python packages — currently lazy-imported, not pre-installed). Foundation phase in v1.18.0; live activation pending community-tester validation. If push is unavailable, the integration silently falls back to polling. Reload the integration after toggling.
data_description.enable_push_fcm: EXPERIMENTAL: CUPRA/SEAT only. Enables real-time Firebase Cloud Messaging push from OLA backend (requires firebase-messaging Python package — same lazy-import as v1.18.0 Skoda MQTT toggle). Foundation phase in v1.19.0; live activation pending community-tester validation. If push is unavailable, the integration silently falls back to polling. Reload the integration after toggling.
data_description.enable_push_audi_vw: Live (beta): Audi/VW only. Enables real-time push notifications from the Cariad FCM channel (same one myAudi/WeConnect mobile apps use, covers lock/unlock results, charging-state, climate, alarm). Decoded events are fired onto the Home Assistant event bus as ``vag_connect_push_event`` and trigger a coordinator refresh. Requires firebase-messaging Python package (same lazy-import as Skoda MQTT + CUPRA/SEAT FCM). Live activation since v2.8.0 (Action #4); the credential resolver is still tester-gated until task #40 follow-up lands the Cariad Firebase sender_id. If push is unavailable, the integration silently falls back to polling. Reload the integration after toggling.
data_description.wake_before_poll: When enabled, the integration sends a wake POST to the vehicle before fetching status for any VIN that was OFFLINE on the previous poll. The car receives a server-side wake-up and pushes fresh data, then the integration waits the wake delay before the status read. Closes the 'all sensors unknown for parked cars' gap but costs one extra API call per offline VIN per poll cycle. Off by default. Reload the integration after toggling.
data_description.wake_delay_seconds: Seconds to wait between the wake-up POST and the status fetch. The default of 15 seconds is what most brand backends need to ingest the wake-induced data push. Lower values risk reading stale data; higher values stretch the poll cycle without further benefit.
data_description.client_id_override: Power-user escape hatch for when the community spots a fresh OAuth client_id in a new VW Group APK before our daily atlas builder catches it. Paste the full UUID@apps_vw-dilab_com string here and the resolver tries it FIRST on every authenticate, with all the existing client_ids still in the chain as fallback. Leave empty for default behaviour. Format: 8-4-4-4-12 hex digits followed by @apps_vw-dilab_com. Malformed values are silently ignored.
data_description.eu_data_act_auto_kickoff: Only meaningful when the integration is operating in read-only EU Data Act portal mode (live BFF strategies have failed). When ON, the coordinator checks for an active 15-min Custom Data Request per VIN at startup and creates one when none exists. The kickoff implies a 1-month data subscription on your account at the portal, so this is opt-in. The portal accepts AT MOST ONE active custom request per VIN at a time. Off by default.
data_description.enable_data_act_browser: EXPERIMENTAL: only meaningful when the integration has fallen back to the EU Data Act portal (read-only). Drives a headless Chromium via the optional 'playwright' Python package to click the portal's data-export button automatically. The package is NOT preinstalled (it pulls roughly 100 MB of Chromium). When enabled but missing, a Repair issue tells you how to install it from inside the Home Assistant container. Reload the integration after toggling.

two_factor_required.title: Two-factor authentication required — {brand}
two_factor_required.description:
Your {brand} account ({username}) requires an email verification code.

**One-time fix:**
1. Open the **{brand} app** on your phone
2. Sign in manually
3. Confirm the code sent by email
4. Restart Home Assistant

After this one-time step, the integration stores the login token — you won't need to repeat this.

email_two_factor_required.title: Email 2FA — check your inbox
email_two_factor_required.description:
Your {brand} account ({username}) requires an **email verification code**.

**One-time fix:**
1. **Check your email inbox** (and spam folder) for a 6-digit code from VAG IDP
2. Open the **{brand} app** on your phone
3. Sign in manually and enter the code
4. Restart Home Assistant

After this one-time step, the integration stores the login token — you won't need to repeat this.

terms_and_conditions.title: Accept terms and conditions — {brand}
terms_and_conditions.description:
There are new terms and conditions for {brand} ({username}).

1. Open the {brand} app
2. Sign in and accept the terms
3. Restart Home Assistant

marketing_consent.title: Consent required — {brand}
marketing_consent.description:
A new privacy consent is required for {brand} ({username}).

Open app → Profile → Consents → confirm → restart HA.

too_many_requests.title: Account temporarily suspended — {brand}
too_many_requests.description:
The {brand} account ({username}) is temporarily suspended due to too many requests.

Please **wait 15 minutes** and restart Home Assistant.
Tip: Increase the update interval to at least 15 minutes.

auth_failed.title: Authentication failed — {brand}
auth_failed.description:
Connection to {brand} ({username}) failed.

Settings → Devices & Services → VW Group Connect → ⋮ → Reconfigure.

vehicle_data_scout_findings.title: Vehicle Data Scout — {count} new field(s) on {brand}
vehicle_data_scout_findings.description:
The Vehicle Data Scout detected **{count}** new field(s) in the {brand} API response that VW Group Connect doesn't read yet.

This usually means your vehicle has a feature or firmware version we haven't mapped — your help would teach the integration to support it.

**1-click report:**
📤 Click **Learn more** below to open a pre-filled GitHub issue.

**For Facebook / forum:** open Settings → Devices → VW Group Connect → Diagnostics → Download — paste the masked report.

_Privacy: VINs masked, GPS rounded to 1 decimal, userIDs/JWTs/emails stripped. Nothing is sent automatically._

error_reporter_findings.title: Error Reporter — {count} recent error(s) on {brand}
error_reporter_findings.description:
The Error Reporter captured **{count}** recent integration error(s) for {brand}.

Reporting them helps us find bugs that only show up on specific vehicles or firmware versions.

**1-click report:**
📤 Click **Learn more** below to open a pre-filled GitHub issue.

**For Facebook / forum:** open Settings → Devices → VW Group Connect → Diagnostics → Download — the report is included with VINs and tokens masked.

_Privacy: VINs masked, Bearer/JWT/UUID/email tokens stripped from messages and tracebacks. No credentials are included._

quota_low.title: API quota low — {remaining} requests remaining today
quota_low.description:
Your VAG account is approaching the daily API request limit ({remaining} of {limit} remaining, {pct}%).

To protect against quota lockout (the manufacturer backend can soft-suspend the account when the daily cap is reached), consider:

1. **Increase the update interval** — Settings → Devices & Services → VW Group Connect → Configure → Update Interval (currently typically 5–10 min)
2. **Reduce other VAG integrations** running on the same account (each counts against the same quota)
3. **Wait for daily reset** — usually around midnight in the manufacturer's home time zone

This warning auto-clears when the remaining count recovers.

quota_critical.title: API quota CRITICAL — {remaining} requests remaining today
quota_critical.description:
Your VAG account has fewer than 25 API requests remaining today ({remaining} of {limit}, {pct}%). The manufacturer backend may soft-suspend the account if you exhaust the budget.

**Immediate action:**
1. **Pause polling** — Settings → Devices & Services → VW Group Connect → Configure → Update Interval → set to 30 min or higher
2. Avoid manual refreshes / vehicle wake-ups until the budget resets

This warning auto-clears when the remaining count recovers.

account_locked.title: {brand} account appears to be temporarily locked
account_locked.description:
The brand backend has returned **3 lock-class auth responses** in the last 30 minutes (most recently HTTP {last_status}). This typically means VW has temporarily blocked the underlying brand account after too many failed token-refresh attempts.

The lock usually lifts within **a few hours up to 24 hours**. The integration has stopped retrying so the lock window does not extend.

**While you wait**:

1. **Do not** sign in / out of the manufacturer app repeatedly — that extends the lock.
2. Wait at least 1-2 hours, then open the official manufacturer app on your phone. If the app signs you in cleanly, the backend has unlocked your account.
3. Once unlocked, raise the `scan_interval` in Settings → Devices & Services → VW Group Connect → Configure to 15-30 minutes to reduce future pressure.
4. Optional: enable the read-only EU Data Act portal mode via the OptionsFlow to keep some data flowing during future incidents.

This warning auto-clears the first time the next token refresh succeeds.

data_act_no_data.title: EU Data Act portal: no vehicle data yet ({brand})
data_act_no_data.description:
The EU Data Act portal signed in for {brand} but isn't returning any vehicle data yet.

Usual causes:

1. **A VW-side outage.** VW reported an all-brands EU Data Act portal outage from late May 2026 (their notice: the data-extraction infrastructure failed under load). While it lasts the portal returns empty data for every tool, not just this integration, and it has been recovering brand by brand.
2. **No data request set up.** The portal only delivers once you create a *continuous* data request for your car.
3. **The car hasn't sent anything new.** A fresh dataset is only published after the car has been driven, charged, or locked/unlocked.

**Quickest check:** open `eu-data-act.drivesomethinggreater.com` in a browser and sign in. If you see no data for your car there either, it's the VW side — nothing this integration can do until VW restores it. If there is no data request, create a continuous (15-minute) one, tick all data clusters, then drive or wake the car and wait an hour or two.

This is the read-only portal beta — the warning clears by itself once data arrives.

data_act_browser_missing.description:
You enabled the headless-browser fallback for the EU Data Act portal but the `playwright` Python package is not installed in your Home Assistant container.

**To install:** open the HA container shell (Add-ons → Terminal & SSH, or `docker exec` for a manual install) and run:

```
pip install playwright
playwright install chromium
```

The chromium download is large (around 100 MB), which is why this dependency is opt-in instead of bundled with the integration. After installing, restart Home Assistant.

Alternatively, disable the toggle under Settings → Devices & Services → VW Group Connect → Configure → 'EU Data Act portal: headless-browser fallback'.
data_act_browser_missing.title: EU Data Act portal: optional 'playwright' package missing

data_act_session_expired.description:
The {brand} portal at `eu-data-act.drivesomethinggreater.com` returned HTTP 401 to a Custom Data Request API call, which means the session cookies have expired. The portal does not support background token refresh, so Home Assistant cannot recover this automatically.

**What to do:** Settings → Devices & Services → VW Group Connect → 3-dot menu → Reconfigure → re-enter your credentials. The next setup cycle will reopen the portal session and the integration will resume polling the active Custom Data Request.

This warning auto-clears as soon as the next portal API call succeeds.
data_act_session_expired.title: EU Data Act portal session expired - reauthenticate

ola_headers_outdated.description:
VW Group's online-services backend (OLA) has refused **{count}** requests in a row for {brand} with HTTP 403 Forbidden.

This is **not** an outdated-header or app-version problem, and it's not something you can fix by reconfiguring or re-logging in. VW appears to have restricted online-services access for {brand} (and SEAT) on their side — the official app uses a separate, fully-trusted access path we can't reproduce, so a header bump or app-version override won't bring it back.

Where it can, the integration falls back to the read-only EU Data Act portal for whatever data is still available. {brand} data should return on its own once VW restores access. Nothing to do on your end — this warning auto-clears when requests start succeeding again.
ola_headers_outdated.title: {brand} online services blocked on VW's side — {count} consecutive 403 errors

start_climate_control.name: Start Climate Control (Rich)
start_climate_control.description: Start pre-conditioning with individual seat heaters, glass heating, climate mode and at-unlock behaviour. Only applies to Audi and VW EU (CARIAD BFF). Other brands fall through to the basic `start_climatisation` service. All payload fields except `device_id` are optional - omitted fields keep the brand default.

device_id.name: Vehicle
device_id.description: Vehicle that receives the pre-conditioning command.

temp_c.name: Target temperature (C)
temp_c.description: Cabin target temperature in degrees Celsius (15 to 30, step 0.5).

glass_heating.name: Glass heating
glass_heating.description: Enable windscreen and rear-window heating alongside the cabin pre-conditioning.

seat_fl.name: Front-left seat heating
seat_fl.description: Enable seat heating on the front-left seat.

seat_fr.name: Front-right seat heating
seat_fr.description: Enable seat heating on the front-right seat.

seat_rl.name: Rear-left seat heating
seat_rl.description: Enable seat heating on the rear-left seat.

seat_rr.name: Rear-right seat heating
seat_rr.description: Enable seat heating on the rear-right seat.

climatisation_at_unlock.name: Continue climate when unlocked
climatisation_at_unlock.description: Keep pre-conditioning running after the vehicle is unlocked.

climatisation_mode.name: Climatisation mode
climatisation_mode.description: Comfort (full power) or economy (reduced power, longer runtime).

update_charging_settings.name: Update Charging Settings (SEAT/CUPRA)
update_charging_settings.description: Update the SEAT/CUPRA charge plan in one call. Sets any combination of target state of charge, maximum AC charge current and the auto-unlock-when-charged flag. Other brands are rejected at the coordinator dispatch level.

vin.name: VIN
vin.description: VIN of the vehicle to update.

target_soc.name: Target state of charge (%)
target_soc.description: Target state of charge between 20 and 100 percent.

auto_unlock_charge.name: Auto-unlock plug when fully charged
auto_unlock_charge.description: When true the connector unlocks itself once charging is complete.

execute_vehicle_action.name: Execute Vehicle Action
execute_vehicle_action.description: Unified service that dispatches a vehicle action selected from a dropdown. Equivalent to calling the per-action service of the same name but easier to find in the Home Assistant Lovelace service picker. All existing per-action services (vag_connect.lock, vag_connect.start_climatisation, etc) keep working unchanged.

device_id.name: Vehicle
device_id.description: The vehicle device to act on.

open_app.description: Fires the `vag_connect_open_app` event on the HA event bus with payload fields `vin`, `brand`, `deeplink_url` and `action` so a Lovelace card can open the brand's native mobile app (myAudi, WeConnect, MyŠkoda, MySEAT, MyCUPRA, My Porsche or VW US) on the calling device. The integration cannot open URLs on a phone by itself; the dashboard card is responsible for navigating `window.location.href = deeplink_url` on iOS / Android.

action.description: Optional path segment appended to the deeplink so the brand app routes directly to a section. Default `open` opens the app to its home screen.
action.name: App action

vin.description: VIN of the vehicle whose brand-app should open.
vin.name: VIN

open_app.name: Open native brand app (deeplink)

vehicle_not_found.message: Vehicle with VIN {vin} not found. Check that the integration is started.

spin_required.message: S-PIN is required for this action. Configure it under Settings → Devices & Services → VW Group Connect → Configure.

feature_not_supported.message: The feature {feature} is not supported by your vehicle or the current API client. The action was not sent.

wake_budget_exhausted.message: Daily wake-up budget exhausted ({count}/{budget}). To protect the 12V battery, no further wake-up requests are sent until midnight UTC. See sensor.wake_count_today.

wake_cooldown_active.message: Wake-up cooldown active ({remaining_s}s remaining). To protect the 12V battery, wake-ups are limited to one every {cooldown_min} minutes per vehicle.

read_only_mode_active.message: Read-only mode is enabled in the integration options. Disable it under Settings → Devices & Services → VW Group Connect → Configure if you want to send vehicle commands.
